description: Phase 2.5 corpus for citation-verifier refactor v0.3 status taxonomy. Each fixture declares an input citation and the expected verifier result under the six-status taxonomy in models.Status. Consumed by Phase 3 acceptance tests. See tests/data/refactor_corpus_survey.md for provenance and selection criteria.
selection_criteria: Each of the six statuses has at least 5 fixtures (hard minimum).
The named exemplars from design v2 §4 (Koch, Gilliam, Menges, WRONG_CASE, VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE) are all present and tagged with category 'named_exemplar'.
Primary source: ~/Projects/case-law-proposition-benchmark/scratch/cl-coverage-offshoot/ — 250 ground-truthed citations with hand-classified diagnoses.
Secondary sources: tests/data/known_real_citations.json (anchors), tests/data/known_fake_citations.json (WRONG_CASE + hallucinated NOT_FOUND).
VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE fixtures are synthetic (mock_spec field declares the simulated failure mode; Phase 3 builds the mock harness).
Provisional classifications use phase3_classification_open: true so Phase 3 can rule without re-curating.
expected_warnings_subset uses subset semantics — Phase 3 may add other warning categories without breaking the fixture.

fixtures.citation: Obergefell v. Hodges, 576 U.S. 644 (2015)
fixtures.rationale: Landmark SCOTUS case; primary citation_lookup resolves cleanly. Anchor fixture for the standard VERIFIED happy path.

fixtures.citation: Bossart v. King Cnty., Case No. 2:24-cv-01776-JHC, 2025 WL 459154, at *1 (W.D. Wash. Feb. 11, 2025)
fixtures.rationale: Pre-Phase-3 xfail (CL cluster-ID drift). Phase 3's caption_investigation may rescue. expected_final_ids.cluster_id pinned to the originally-canonical ID; Phase 3 confirms or updates. Abbreviation normalization (Cnty.) needed in the resolution path.

fixtures.citation: Busha v. SC Dep't of Mental Health, No. 6:18-CV-02337-DCC, 2019 WL 651680 (D.S.C. Feb. 13, 2019)
fixtures.rationale: Pre-Phase-3 xfail (CL cluster-ID drift). Phase 3's caption_investigation may rescue. Dep't -> Department abbreviation normalization + judge suffix stripping. cluster_id pinned to known_real_citations.json value; Phase 3 confirms or updates.

fixtures.citation: Townsley v. Lifewise Assurance Co., Case No. C15-1228-JCC, 2016 WL 1393548, at *3 (W.D. Wash. April 8, 2016)
fixtures.rationale: Pre-Phase-3 xfail (CL cluster-ID drift). Phase 3's caption_investigation may rescue. Docket number shorthand expansion (C15 -> 15-cv) + judge suffix. CL has different defendant name. cluster_id pinned to known_real_citations.json value; Phase 3 confirms or updates.

fixtures.citation: Anderson v. Furst, No. 17-cv-12676, 2018 WL 4407750, at *2 (E.D. Mich. Sept. 17, 2018)
fixtures.rationale: Pre-Phase-3 xfail (CL cluster-ID drift). RECAP with exact date matching. Phase 3's caption_investigation may rescue. cluster_id pinned to known_real_citations.json value; Phase 3 confirms or updates.

fixtures.citation: Hanover Shoe, Inc. v. United Shoe Machinery Corp., 392 U.S. 481 (1968)
fixtures.rationale: SCOTUS happy-path sample. citation_lookup resolves on first call. Source pool: benchmark in_cl_via_citation_lookup (45 SCOTUS rows).

fixtures.citation: Steir v. Girl Scouts of the USA, 383 F.3d 7, 12 (1st Cir. 2004)
fixtures.rationale: Circuit (1st Cir.) happy-path sample. citation_lookup resolves on first call. Source pool: benchmark in_cl_via_citation_lookup (43 Circuit rows).

fixtures.citation: Janvey v. Proskauer Rose, LLP, 59 F. Supp. 3d 1, 7 (D.D.C. 2014)
fixtures.rationale: Federal District happy-path sample. citation_lookup resolves on first call. Source pool: benchmark in_cl_via_citation_lookup (18 Federal_District rows).

fixtures.citation: People v. Howery, 178 Ill. 2d 1, 32 (Ill. 1997)
fixtures.rationale: State court of last resort (Illinois Supreme) happy-path sample. citation_lookup resolves on first call. Source pool: benchmark in_cl_via_citation_lookup (38 State_COLR rows).

fixtures.citation: Peerenboom v. Marvel Entertainment, LLC, 148 A.D.3d 531 (N.Y. App. Div. 2017)
fixtures.rationale: State intermediate appellate court (NY App. Div.) happy-path sample where the A.D.3d cite IS in CL's citation index — contrasts with the cl_cluster_parallel_cite_missing VERIFIED_PARTIAL fixtures. Source pool: benchmark in_cl_via_citation_lookup (26 State_IAC rows).

fixtures.citation: Isaacs v. Caterpillar, Inc., 702 F. Supp. 711, 713 (S.D. Ohio 1988)
fixtures.rationale: Bonus Federal District sample exercising abbreviation expansion in the case name (Inc.). citation_lookup resolves on first call. Source pool: benchmark in_cl_via_citation_lookup.

fixtures.citation: Occidental Permian, Ltd. v. Citation 2002 Inv. LLC, 689 S.W.3d 899, 905 (Tex. 2024)
fixtures.rationale: Cluster exists in CL but its citations[] field is empty — primary citation_lookup misses, opinion_search fuzzy fallback resolves to the same cluster. Sub-case of VERIFIED via fallback path. Resolving stage is opinion_search, not citation_lookup.

fixtures.citation: Sundown Energy LP v. HJSA No. 3, L.P., 622 S.W.3d 884, 889 (Tex. 2021)
fixtures.rationale: Cluster exists in CL but its citations[] field is empty — primary citation_lookup misses, opinion_search fuzzy fallback resolves. Second cl_cluster_citations_empty sample for sub-case variety.

fixtures.citation: Bloomberg L.P. v. Bd. of Govs. of the Fed. Reserve Sys., 649 F. 3d 651, 657 (D.C. Cir. 2011)
fixtures.rationale: Court-confirmed hallucinated citation from a sanctioned AI-generated brief (Gonzalez v. TTRA, S.D. Tex. 2025). Plausible D.C. Cir. format; no case exists.

fixtures.citation: Head v. Chicora Life Ctr., LLC, 290 F. Supp. 3d 883, 893 (S.D. Tex. 2018)
fixtures.rationale: Court-confirmed hallucinated citation from the same Gonzalez v. TTRA sanctions order. Fabricated.

fixtures.citation: Gibbs v. Wright, 2020 WL 1877661, at *2 (E.D. Tenn. Apr. 15, 2020)
fixtures.rationale: Court-confirmed hallucinated WL cite from Hardy v. Ford Motor Co. sanctions discussion. Fabricated.

fixtures.citation: Terry Black's Barbecue, L.L.C. v. State Auto. Mut. Ins. Co., 22 F.4th 450, 459 (5th Cir. 2022)
fixtures.rationale: Benchmark-classified not_in_cl: case is real but absent from CL's index across all stages. Useful as a non-hallucinated NOT_FOUND fixture (real case, just not in CL).

fixtures.citation: Azad v. 270 5th Realty Corp., 10 N.Y.3d 706 (N.Y. 2008)
fixtures.rationale: Benchmark-classified not_in_cl: NY Court of Appeals memorandum decision absent from CL's index. Real case, not in CL.

fixtures.citation: People v. Campbell, 243 A.D.3d 1221 (N.Y. App. Div. 2024)
fixtures.rationale: Benchmark-classified not_in_cl: NY App Div decision absent from CL's index. Real case, not in CL.

fixtures.citation: Iglesias v. City of Hialeah, 305 So. 3d 20 (Fla. Dist. Ct. App. 2020)
fixtures.rationale: Benchmark-classified rescue_was_false_positive: pre-Phase-3 fallback ladder rescued this incorrectly. Phase 3 stricter logic should return NOT_FOUND. Exploratory; phase3_classification_open: true until confirmed.

fixtures.citation: Gilliard v. McWilliams, 2019 WL 3304707
fixtures.rationale: Rule 25(d) automatic-substitution diverges the brief caption from CL's display caption (same case, different display name). citation_lookup resolves the WL cite cleanly; the name matcher must flag the mismatch as cl_display_name_data_bug rather than escalating to WRONG_CASE.

fixtures.citation: Preston v. Smith, 2023 WL 5337430
fixtures.rationale: Rule 25(d) substitution case. citation_lookup resolves; CL's display caption diverges from the brief caption. Second Rule 25(d) sample for sub-case variety.

fixtures.citation: Viken Detection Corp. v. Doe, 2019 WL 5268725
fixtures.rationale: Rule 25(d) substitution case. citation_lookup resolves; CL's display caption diverges from the brief caption. Third Rule 25(d) sample (all 3 from benchmark).

fixtures.citation: Michael B. v. Berryhill, 2019 WL 2269962
fixtures.rationale: Social Security Administration case using a plaintiff pseudonym (Michael B.) in the brief caption. CL stores the real plaintiff name (Buschman v. Berryhill). Same case, different display name. The name matcher must flag as cl_display_name_data_bug rather than WRONG_CASE.

fixtures.citation: John S. v. Bisignano, 2025 WL 1505405
fixtures.rationale: SSA case using a plaintiff pseudonym (John S.). CL stores the real plaintiff name (Sims v. Bisignano). Second ssa_pseudonym sample.

fixtures.citation: Koch v. United States, 857 F.3d 267 (5th Cir. 2017)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named exemplar. Live citation_lookup against 857 F.3d 267 returns cluster 4390987 with CL case_name 'Ricky Koch v. Tote, Incorporated' — divergent from the brief's 'Koch v. United States' caption. The verifier's name matcher flags the mismatch; Phase 3's caption_investigation confirms it IS the same case (CL data-bug, not a wrong case).

fixtures.citation: Gold v. Wallace, 230 A.D.3d 1113, 2024 NY Slip Op 04376 (N.Y. App. Div. 2024)
fixtures.rationale: NY A.D.3d primary cite (230 A.D.3d 1113) is not in CL's citation index. The parallel NY Slip Op cite (2024 NY Slip Op 04376) resolves to cluster 10114438. Status: VERIFIED_PARTIAL — the case is verified, but coverage is incomplete because the primary cite didn't resolve.

fixtures.citation: Hersko v. Hersko, 224 A.D.3d 810, 2024 NY Slip Op 00894 (N.Y. App. Div. 2024)
fixtures.rationale: NY A.D.3d primary cite (224 A.D.3d 810) is not in CL's citation index. Parallel NY Slip Op (2024 NY Slip Op 00894) resolves to cluster 9477252. Second parallel_cite_ny_adv sample.

fixtures.citation: People v. Dondorfer, 235 A.D.3d 71, 2024 NY Slip Op 06432 (N.Y. App. Div. 2024)
fixtures.rationale: A.D.3d primary cite (235 A.D.3d 71) not in CL's index. Parallel slip op (2024 NY Slip Op 06432) resolves to cluster 10298541. Third parallel_cite_ny_adv sample.

fixtures.citation: People v. Kumar, 242 A.D.3d 1231, 2025 NY Slip Op 05977 (N.Y. App. Div. 2025)
fixtures.rationale: A.D.3d primary cite (242 A.D.3d 1231) not in CL's index. Parallel slip op (2025 NY Slip Op 05977) resolves to cluster 10717850. Fourth parallel_cite_ny_adv sample.

fixtures.citation: People v. Walker, 228 A.D.3d 1318, 2024 NY Slip Op 03278 (N.Y. App. Div. 2024)
fixtures.rationale: A.D.3d primary cite (228 A.D.3d 1318) not in CL's index. Parallel slip op (2024 NY Slip Op 03278) resolves to cluster 9880367. Fifth parallel_cite_ny_adv sample (all 5 from benchmark).

fixtures.citation: Gilliam v. Uni Holdings, 201 A.D.3d 83, 88-89, 2021 NY Slip Op 06798 (N.Y. App. Div. 2021)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named exemplar. A.D.3d primary cite (201 A.D.3d 83) not in CL's citation index. Parallel slip op (2021 NY Slip Op 06798) resolves to cluster 5305052 with CL case_name 'Gilliam v. Uni holdings'. Note: CL also has a duplicate cluster 5304049 for the same case_name + date_filed; Phase 3's deduper picks one canonical cluster. Status: VERIFIED_PARTIAL.
